title: cnidoscolus palmeri
intro: Cnidoscolus palmeri, also known as the coyotillo or catclaw, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and Central America. It is a member of the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive catclaw-like thorns and edible leaves, stems, and roots. The medicinal properties of Cnidoscolus palmeri include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Cnidoscolus palmeri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and protect against oxidative stress.
